metadata.name: Website Browser Testing Loop
metadata.description: Run a read-only browser-based website test, capture screenshots and console/network evidence, review raw findings, and produce a confirmed bug list.

objective.user_goal: Test a website with optional administrator credentials and produce an evidence-backed bug list.
objective.business_value: Turn broad website testing requests into bounded browser probes that save tokens by storing evidence on disk and reviewing only distilled findings.
objective.done_definition: A loop run folder contains browser evidence, reviewed bug-list.md, residual-risk notes, and terminal verification confirming artifacts exist and secrets were not written to files.
non_goals: Do not create, update, approve, delete, publish, export sensitive data, or submit business forms.
Do not perform load testing, vulnerability exploitation, destructive security testing, or credential changes.
Do not claim coverage of backend source code, database health, host health, or microservice internals.

ambiguity_triggers: test depth requires production data change
captcha, MFA, or session challenge blocks login

secondary_user_query.prompt: Should this browser test stay read-only, or may it create controlled test data in a non-production environment?
required_when: a defect can only be confirmed by mutating production data
a destructive or irreversible action would be required

assumption_policy.allowed: Assume read-only, non-destructive browser testing is authorized; record all skipped mutation paths as residual risk.

block_if: test requires bypassing authentication or authorization
privacy-sensitive data would need to be exported

split_until: each route or workflow probe has a single observable acceptance criterion
each reported bug has reproduction steps and evidence artifact references
each task fits inside the browser evidence and token budget
decomposition_policy.minimum_task_granularity: one login, navigation, route, form-validation, console/network, or reporting check with a deterministic artifact
decomposition_policy.dependency_policy: parents cannot complete until child probes and their artifact checks pass

loop_nodes.purpose: Coordinate read-only admin website testing and evidence-backed bug reporting.
entry_conditions: target_url and credential reference are available

children.purpose: Probe visible admin workflows, capture artifacts, and iterate only while new high-value evidence appears.
Promote only evidence-backed findings into the final bug list and separate uncertain leads.
exit_conditions: uncertain findings labeled as residual risk

atomic_tasks.goal: Record the target, credential reference, read-only boundary, and blocked mutation actions.
acceptance_criteria: state.json does not contain plaintext password

atomic_tasks.goal: Open the site, authenticate using the environment-backed credential, and capture app shell evidence without changing data.
acceptance_criteria: login result is recorded as success, blocked, or failed with evidence

atomic_tasks.goal: Probe visible routes, empty/error/loading states, and console/network failures from the authenticated app shell.
acceptance_criteria: at least three reachable protected surfaces are checked when available
console errors and failed network requests are captured
blocked routes are labeled as automation/session boundary unless manually confirmed

atomic_tasks.goal: Write a reproducible bug list from browser evidence and separate unconfirmed leads from confirmed defects.
acceptance_criteria: each confirmed bug includes severity, reproduction steps, expected behavior, actual behavior, evidence, and repair direction
uncertain findings are not promoted to confirmed bugs

allowed_when: console/network review can be separated from visual workflow review
disallowed_when: multiple workers would use the same authenticated session destructively
a worker asks to mutate production data
subagent_activation.token_budget_policy: workers return distilled evidence summaries under 2000 tokens with artifact paths instead of raw logs

context_policy.tool_output_trimming: store screenshots, HAR-like summaries, console error summaries, route names, and reproduction steps; avoid raw noisy logs

compaction.summary_contract: preserve target, safety boundary, completed probes, confirmed defects, artifacts, skipped routes, and exit reason

success: bug-list.md exists and passes quality checks
failure: target cannot be reached after two attempts
login cannot proceed because of MFA/captcha/session challenge

verifiers.command: test -s evidence/browser-summary.md && find evidence -type f | grep -q .

anti_gaming_rules: do not mark unconfirmed leads as confirmed defects
do not store credentials in files
do not bypass login or authorization

cost_policy.optimization_goal: maximize verified browser evidence per token while avoiding repeated route re-reading and raw log dumping

forbidden_behaviors: claiming automation login failure as site failure without evidence

portability_policy.platform_selection_query: Should this browser-testing loop run in Codex, Claude Code, Cursor, or portable mode?

failure_modes.mitigation: record as automation/session boundary unless a manual logged-in browser reproduces the same issue
store raw artifacts on disk and summarize only signatures, routes, and failing evidence
require reproduction steps and artifact references before confirmed status
human gate before any mutation, export, approval, deletion, or permission change
stop when visited route, screenshot count, and finding fingerprints stop changing

irreversible_actions: create, update, approve, delete, publish, or export business data
change credentials, roles, permissions, or tenant settings
run load, destructive security, or exploit testing

escalation.condition: Login challenge, mutation requirement, privacy-sensitive data exposure, no-progress, or budget exit is reached.
escalation.message_template: Website bug-hunt loop stopped: {reason}. Evidence: {evidence_ref}. Needed decision: {open_question}.
